# R0 · Overview — Redis Patterns Applied

> Orientation before the patterns: Redis Patterns Applied to the BCS architecture — EchoMQ backed by Valkey,
> Valkey under the hood, EchoStore in front — and the door to the pattern chapters R1–R8. Served at
> /redis-patterns/overview.

## Modules

- [R0.2 · Valkey under codemojex](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game): the placement — codemojex reaches one boundary, the echo data layer; below it Valkey serves two roles, EchoStore (the near-cache) and the EchoMQ bus (the owned-protocol bus at echo/apps/echo_mq), both over one owned client, EchoWire; no surface holds a raw Valkey key.
  - [The facade seam](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game/the-facade-seam): every surface calls only the echo data layer and reads a typed result from a closed set, never a raw store condition; EchoWire is the one owned Valkey client below the seam.
  - [The two roles](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game/two-roles): EchoStore, the derived near-cache, vs the EchoMQ bus, the authoritative state — and which pattern families ground in each.
  - [The reserved tier](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game/reserved-tier): the tier once held in reserve, now shipped — EchoMQ at echo/apps/echo_mq, the convergence target of the EchoMQ program; the worked move is the real claim script.
- [R0.3 · Patterns become protocol](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol): the four-layer model and the immutable core; the data model as the cross-runtime contract; the door to the EchoMQ course.
  - [The four layers](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol/the-four-layers): immutable L1/L2 vs language-specific L3/L4.
  - [The immutable core](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol/the-immutable-core): the frozen L1/L2 core and its governance.
  - [The door to EchoMQ](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol/the-door-to-echomq): the cross-runtime contract and the forward door.

## Up

- [Course home](/redis-patterns) · [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching)
